element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial-style introduction to trap-escape mechanics; player learns basic problem-solving under time pressure with non-lethal consequences
Quick button response combat integrated throughout; escalates as player progresses through Metro City

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game streets of Metro City
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Detective work and clue collection; non-violent exploration mechanic
Performance feedback system; good/poor deeds affect meter; gates access to new areas based on reputation
Four escapable deathtraps (James Bond-inspired); each has a solution; player escapes with popularity penalty
Inescapable trap; forces player restart from beginning; escalates consequence severity
Popularity-based area unlock; incentivizes good performance and replays

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to streets after trap sequence
typical_tension_triggers: Popularity meter decrease from poor performance
Area access denial due to low popularity
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension through repeating trap-escape pattern; four escapable deathtraps punctuate mid-to-late game; escalating consequence severity; comedy tone lightens tension; investigation phases provide breathing room between action sequences

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Higher popularity unlocks new accessible areas of Metro City; encourages revisiting streets with expanded options

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low-to-moderate; opening bomb escape teaches mechanics with non-lethal stakes; detective work non-threatening; introduction to popularity system
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate; escalating combat encounters; first four deathtraps introduce high-stakes escape sequences with solvable solutions; popularity-based gating creates replay incentive
difficulty_curve.late_game: High; final inescapable deathtrap removes safety net; failure forces complete restart from game beginning; ultimate consequence escalation
notable_spikes: Game start bomb defusal - sudden time pressure
First deathtrap encounter - introduces escape puzzle mechanic
Final deathtrap - no solution available; forces restart

notable_level_patterns.description: Structured trap-solving sections where Nightshade must escape before timer expires. First four traps have solutions and return player to Metro City streets with popularity loss. Final trap is inescapable, requiring game restart. James Bond film-inspired design creates narrative structure in absence of battery-backed saves.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Opening bomb-defusal; four mid-to-late game deathtraps; final inescapable trap

notable_level_patterns.description: Point-and-click detective phases between action sequences. Player explores city, collects clues, performs deeds. Popularity meter directly gates access to new areas, creating incentive-based spatial progression.

Popularity meter controls which Metro City areas remain accessible. Good performance unlocks new regions; poor performance restricts access. Creates natural replayability and encourages non-violent solutions.
